title: Client
description:
A client represents an organisation or service that sends communications.
    Client records are promoted through DRAFT, INT, PROD, and DISABLED states, while environment-specific operational settings and global routing queues are embedded as child configuration collections.

slug.title: Client Slug
slug.description: Human-readable identifier for the client, used alongside the UUID client ID.

status.title: EnvironmentStatus
status.description: Indicates whether the configuration is in draft, enabled in INT, enabled in PROD, or disabled in all environments. `PROD` implies the configuration has also been promoted through INT.

maxRequestsPerSecond.description: The maximum number of requests that can be made by the client in one second (TPS or Transactions Per Second).

messageRequestsMeshMailboxes.description: Environment-scoped MESH mailbox settings for message request workflows.

items.title: MessageRequestsMeshMailbox
items.description: Environment-scoped MESH mailbox configuration used for message request traffic.

workflowIdPrefix.description: Workflow identifier prefix used for message request exchanges in the target environment.

digitalLettersMeshMailboxes.description: Environment-scoped Digital Letters MESH mailbox settings.

items.title: DigitalLettersMeshMailbox
items.description: Environment-scoped MESH mailbox configuration used for Digital Letters integrations.

messageRequestsApimApplications.description: Environment-scoped APIM application identifiers for message request traffic.

applicationId.description: APIM application identifier used to authenticate requests in the target environment.

clientSubscriptions.description: Environment-scoped callback subscription settings for the client.

items.title: ClientSubscription
items.description: Environment-scoped callback configuration for client subscription events.

callbackUrl.description: The URL to which event callbacks should be sent.

callbackApiTokenParameterPath.title: Callback API Token Parameter Store Path
callbackApiTokenParameterPath.description: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store path containing the callback API token used when invoking the client subscription endpoint.

messageStatuses.description: Set of overall message statuses to subscribe to, as documented on https://notify.nhs.uk/using-nhs-notify/message-channel-supplier-status.

items.title: MessageStatus
items.description: Message statuses documented by NHS Notify for overall message progress across all attempted channels.

channelStatuses.description: Set of channel statuses to subscribe to, as documented on https://notify.nhs.uk/using-nhs-notify/message-channel-supplier-status.

items.title: ChannelStatus
items.description: Channel statuses documented by NHS Notify for the progress of an individual channel in a message plan.

supplierStatuses.description: Set of supplier statuses to subscribe to, as documented on https://notify.nhs.uk/using-nhs-notify/message-channel-supplier-status.

items.title: SupplierStatus
items.description: Supplier statuses documented by NHS Notify for detailed supplier-specific outcomes across channels.

globalQueues.description: Queues configured directly on the client for global routing scenarios that are not tied to a campaign.

items.title: Queue
items.description: Campaign queue configuration for a single channel, including per-queue throttling limits and active schedule windows.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: A channel with which a message can be sent

maxRequestsPerSecond.description: Maximum number of requests that may be dispatched per second while the queue throttling rules are active.

maxRequestsPerPeriod.description: Maximum number of requests that may be dispatched during a single quota period while the queue throttling rules are active.

periodSeconds.description: Length of the quota period, in seconds, used with maxRequestsPerPeriod.

periodOffset.description: Offset in seconds applied to the start of each quota period calculation.

schedule.description: Schedule periods during which queue throttling is active. The quota applies whenever the current time falls within any defined schedule period.

items.title: QueueSchedulePeriod
items.description: A scheduled time window during which queue throttling is active. Multiple schedule periods may overlap.

daysOfWeek.description: Days of the week on which this queue schedule period is active.

items.title: DayOfWeek
items.description: Day of the week on which a queue schedule period applies.

startTime.title: Start Time
startTime.description: Inclusive start time for the active schedule period, in HH:MM 24-hour format.

endTime.title: End Time
endTime.description: Exclusive end time for the active schedule period, in HH:MM 24-hour format.

queueNamePrefixOverride.description: Optional prefix override used when generating the queue name for this campaign channel.

items.title: FeatureFlag ID Reference
items.description: Reference to a FeatureFlag by its unique identifier

rfrCodes.title: Reason for Removal Codes
rfrCodes.description:
Reason for removal codes (RFR codes) indicate
why a recipient has been removed from their GP's registered patient list.

This field is optional, but if provided must be an array of valid codes. The default behaviour is to suppress communications to
recipients with any RFR code, but this option can be used to allow recipients with certain codes to receive communications.

See https://data.developer.nhs.uk/dms/mim/6.3.01/Vocabulary/NHAISRemovalReasonCode.htm for information on possible codes.

PDS currently only provides a subset of 'Exit' codes, including those for Deceased patients.
* DEA - Death
* EMB - Embarkation
* SCT - Transferred to Scotland
* NIT - Transferred to Northern Ireland
* TRA - Temporary resident not returned
* ORR - Other reason

items.title: SuppressionFilter
items.description: A suppression filter pattern to exclude communications to certain contact details.

__schema0.title: Environment
__schema0.description: The target environment for an environment-scoped configuration entry.

__schema1.description: MESH mailbox identifier for the target environment.
